---
title: "From Gradients to Correlated Noise"
subtitle: "Building intuition from per-sample gradients through memorization risks to cutting-edge differential privacy mechanisms"
listing:
  contents: posts
  sort: "date asc"
  type: default
  categories: true
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  fields: [image, date, title, subtitle, categories, reading-time]
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: true
---

## The Series

A 6-part journey through the privacy implications of gradient-based learning:

1. **What Gradients Actually Tell a Model** -- the gradient as optimization signal and information carrier
2. How One Data Point Steers the Entire Model -- *coming soon*
3. When Models Remember Too Much -- *coming soon*
4. Differential Privacy: The Math of Plausible Deniability -- *coming soon*
5. The Mechanism Toolkit: Laplace, Gaussian, and DP-SGD -- *coming soon*
6. Correlated Noise: The Frontier of Private Learning -- *coming soon*
